mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding employment and income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns. This mechanism is persistent across the business cycle.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS rises but S&P 500 exhibits a negative return over the subsequent 20 trading days in more than 60% of observed periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a high threshold (8.0%), it indicates severe labor market weakness, raising recession risk and depressing equity valuations. This trigger-based signal predicts negative forward returns.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, the S&P 500 posts positive returns over the next 60 trading days in more than 50% of cases, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflect broad-based demand strength, as consumer spending and housing investment reinforce each other. This dual-positive signal reliably leads to short-term equity gains.
failure_condition: If the combination of rising HOUST and rising RSAFS yields a negative 5-day forward return in >40% of instances, or if the interaction term in a regression fails to add predictive value (coefficient p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In the zero lower bound environment, a rise in the federal funds rate signals that the Fed sees the recovery as self-sustaining, boosting confidence and equity prices. This regime-conditional mechanism only holds when starting from near-zero rates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS monthly level increases from previous month
failure_condition: If, during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and rising, the S&P 500 20-day forward return is negative on average, or if the sign of the relationship flips in a contrary regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in the CPI index (CPIAUCSL) indicates deflationary pressure, which erodes corporate pricing power and increases real debt burdens, leading to negative equity returns. The trigger is a decline exceeding 0.2% in a single month.
failure_condition: If, after a monthly CPI decline >0.2%, the S&P 500 20-day return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or if the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects the fundamental health of the economy. Sustained rises in GDP are associated with higher corporate profits and support equity valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If the correlation between sequential GDPC1 increases and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not positive and statistically significant (Pearson r > 0.10, p < 0.05) over multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a synchronized expansion in manufacturing and labor markets. This combination signals broad economic momentum that boosts short-term stock prices.
failure_condition: If the joint positive move in INDPRO and PAYEMS leads to negative 5-day forward returns in >30% of observations, or if the predictive power of the interaction is not significantly greater than using each series individually.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the federal funds rate (at least 25 basis points over six months) signals an accommodative monetary policy shift, lowering the discount rate on equities and stimulating risk-taking, leading to positive forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decrease by >= 25bp over trailing 6 months
failure_condition: If, following such a rate decline, the average S&P 500 20-day return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or if the hit rate of positive returns is below 60%.
